rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN ZUSCHIN VS. NEW CALIFORNIA LAND COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Renewed Mtn And Renewed Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KATHRYN ZUSCHIN Renewed Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The Court had an opportunity to read and consider Plaintiff's Reply brief, filed December 16, 2011. As such, Plaintiff's request for continuance of the hearing on the motion (e-mailed to the Clerk of Department 302), in order to give the Court time to read the Reply b rief, is denied. The renewed motion is denied. The court granted Plaintiff's previous motion for judgment on the pleadings on October 2, 2011. Plaintiff is not entitled to relief under CCP § 1008. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BRADLEY R. WHITE, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE et al VS. ALBERT K. CLIFFORD AKA AL CLIFFORD, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defts Motion For Summary Judgment Or Alternatively Summary Adjudicaiton; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 9, PLAINTIFF BRADLEY R. WHITE, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE, CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY's Motion To Strike Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication. Granted. Defendants' motion for summary judgment, or alternatively summary adjudication, set for January 13, 2012 is off calendar. Defendants did not properly serve their motion on Plaintiffs pursuant to CCP § 1011(a). Leaving summary judgment papers outside Plaintiffs' office building ("against your door" or "virtually on the street," as described by Defendants' counsel) does not constitute proper service under CCP § 1011(a). Plaintiffs had not agreed to accept service by fax or email. (CCP §§ 1013(e), 1010.6.) Plaintiffs' failure to object to prior service electronically or by fax did not constitute an agreement by Plaintiffs to accept service of summary judgment motion by email and/or fax. In any event, service by email was not timely; the substance of the summary judgment motion did not go through. Defendants' service by mail is untimely. In addition, the motion for summary judgment that Defendants contend they served by hand on Plaintiffs on October 28, 2011 was substantially different than the motion for summary judgment Defendants filed with the Court on November 7, 2011. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ZI QI TAN et al VS. JENNY YING HE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 21, DEFENDANT JENNY HE, REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE FINE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled as to causes of action one through five. CCP 430.10(e) and (f). Defendant Jenny Ying He's actions were pled in sufficient detail to allow Defendant Realty Alliance Inc. dba Century 21 ("Century 21") to answer to the complaint. The First Amended Complaint alleges that Century 21 was in an employment or agency relationship with Defendant He. (FAC 6.) The First Amended Complaint is not uncertain as to Defendant Century 21, and Defendant Century 21 has shown no reason why it cannot reasonably respond to the First Amended Complaint. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for breach of contract. Century 21 argues that Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to support the sixth cause of action, breach of contract. Plaintiff concedes this argument. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ZI QI TAN et al VS. JENNY YING HE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 21, DEFENDANT JENNY HE, REALTY ALLIANCE INC. DBA CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE FINE MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike all requests for emotional distress is granted (paragraphs 22, 29, 34, 40, 46, 53, and Page 9, prayer 1). Emotional distress is not recoverable in cases arising solely out of property damage, and Plaintiff has shown no exception on point. See Elrich v. Menezes, 21 Cal. 4th 543, 554 (1999). Godfry v. Steinpress, 128 Cal. App. 3d. 154 (1982) cited by Plaintiff, is distinguishable on its facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY VS. KEN PAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-501448 WITH CASE CGC-11-512599
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 23, PLAINTIFF WAUKEEN MCCOY MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-501448 WITH CASE CGC-11-512599. Late Tentative Ruling: Denied. As a motion for reconsideration, the motion fails because it is untimely, was filed before the wrong judge, and fails to present any new facts or law warranting reconsideration. See CCP § 1008. Chicago Title's Request for Sanctions is denied. In keeping with the Court's prior orders regarding arbitration and the Court's inherent power to control the proceedings before it, Case 11-512599 is stayed pending determination by the arbitrator in case 501448 which, if any new claims are to be included in the case now being arbitrated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 23, DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Late Tentative Ruling: Denied without prejudice. See ruling on Motion to Consolidate. In keeping with the Court's prior orders regarding arbitration and the Court's inherent power to control the proceedings before it, Case #512599 is stayed pending determination by the arbitrator in case 501448 which, if any new claims are to be included in the case now being arbitrated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VAN GREENFIELD et al VS. AMY VAN PROOYEN AKA AMY VAN PROOYEN GREENFIELD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Of Proceedings And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 24, DEFENDANT AMY VAN PROOYEN AKA AMY VAN PROOYEN GREENFIELD, VAN PROOYEN GREENFIELD LLP's Motion To Stay Of Proceedings And Request For Judicial Notice. The motion is continued to January 10, 2012. The opposing party shall lodge courtesy copies of the opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, EX REL. VS. POPCHIPS, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judicial Approval Of Consent Judgment With Popchips, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 20, 2011, Line 25, PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, EX REL. Notice Of Motion For Judicial Approval Of Consent Judgment With Popchips, Inc. Judge Miller recuses herself. The matter is continued to January 6, 2012 when Judge Kahn will be presiding in Department 302. =(302/MJM)
